summary:
Quantitative assessment of left ventricular regional wall motion can improve after fibrous tissue resection due to removal of restrictive fibroses; this is detectable using echo or MRI with established pre- and post-procedure evaluation methods.

Okay, I need to summarize Agent1’s reasoning based strictly on its content without adding external information.

First sentence should capture whether there's a change — yes, improvement occurs because fibrous tissue restricts movement and removing it allows better mechanical function. Second sentence must highlight how we know — through objective measurement via standard techniques like echocardiography or cardiac MRI which track region-specific contractility over time.

I'll keep both under two full sentences only, focusing solely on what was stated about clinical relevance.
